title: Feature Case Study: FIV World Cup & Continental Championships
scope: The FIV (Fédération Internationale de Viperball) system adds a complete women's international Viperball simulation to the existing CVL and NVL ecosystem. Introduces 68 member nations across 5 confederations, continental championships, cross-confederation playoff, and 32-team World Cup.

delegation_events.decision: Full ViperballEngine for all matches — no fast_sim
delegation_events.quote: Every international match produces full play-by-play, player attribution, drive summaries, and box scores. This was more computationally expensive but essential for the deep stat tracking and box score detail.

delegation_events.decision: Preserve play-by-play in serialization via expanded _slim_result()
delegation_events.quote: The _slim_result() function was expanded to retain drive_summary, play_by_play, and in_game_injuries alongside the standard stats and player_stats.

delegation_events.decision: Single mapping layer, not dual nationality on Player
delegation_events.quote: Rather than modifying the Player dataclass (which would affect all existing systems), the FIV-specific nationality routing lives entirely in engine/fiv.py.

delegation_events.decision: Stats computed from live MatchResult objects — avoid pre-computing redundant stat tables
delegation_events.quote: Tournament stat leaders are computed on-demand from the in-memory FIVCycle object's all_results lists.

actions.description: New engine/fiv.py (~1900 lines): data models, roster generation, tournament engines, rankings, persistence
New data/fiv_nations.json: 68 nations, 5 confederations, tier configs, heritage/diaspora/mercenary tables
ui/api_client.py: typed client methods for all FIV endpoints (+80 lines)
New nicegui_app/pages/international.py: full international UI page (~900 lines)

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end cycle test: 68 teams generated; all 5 continental championships simulated; cross-confederation playoff produced 4 qualifiers; 32-team WC group draw with confederation separation + 48 group matches + 16 knockout matches; Golden Boot and MVP computed correctly; persistence round-trip (save→load→verify) successful; CVL pipeline routed international players to national teams.
validation_claims.check_method: Run FIV engine end-to-end cycle test and verify output counts

section_headers_original: Feature Case Study: FIV World Cup & Continental Championships
UI Design: Best of Both Worlds
unmapped_content: No date appears anywhere in the document (checked: heading, metadata, body). Using null. Title 'Feature Case Study' — not an AAR genre by name; genre_confidence set to low. Architecture details: CVL_STATE_TO_FIV_CODE / CVL_COUNTRY_TO_FIV_CODE mapping layer; _resolve_fiv_code() with fallback logic; NationalTeamPlayer wrapper adds FIV-specific fields without touching core engine. Tier table: Elite (70-95 attrs, 85-95 rating, 1800 Elo: USA/Canada), Strong (60-88, 72-84, 1500: Japan/Sweden/Brazil/Nigeria), Competitive (50-80, 58-71, 1200: Australia/UK/Jamaica/China), Developing (40-72, 40-57, 900: India/Fiji/Haiti/Mongolia). Elo K-factors by competition weight. UI merged design patterns from NVL (maximized dialog, dark gradient, tabbed) and CVL (quarter-by-quarter, drive summary, full play-by-play). CAV 12 nations/6 spots, IFAV 14/6, EVV 16/6, AAV 10/6, CMV 16/4.
parser_notes: No date in document. File titled 'Feature Case Study' not AAR — genre_confidence low. No rationale section (the problem this solves is described in the WVL Galactic Premiership feature report as 'college players graduating into a dead end'). No non_actions or residual risk sections.
